## [00:00 - 03:01]

Basic building block number 11. Why do you look at prices? You know the old saying, if you got to know the price, you can't afford it? That's actually not true. Here's the deal. If you're looking at the price, okay, if you're looking at the price, you have already deceived yourself. The price is not what you're buying. When you go to a restaurant, I did this for years. I was like, a guy told me one day, a guy that was coaching me says, look man, everywhere you go, you look at price. Price is not your problem. I said, what are you talking about, man? I got to get a good deal. I got to make sure I'm not taking advantage of it. He's like, Grant, do you think they're super wealthy, worry about the price of a steak or a cup of coffee? Do you think they worry about the price of almost anything? I said, well, no, dude, they're wealthy. He's like, how do you think they got wealthy, man? I said, what do you mean? They're not worrying about the price, $40 for a book. They're not worried about $1,000 for a program. Their attention is on how to blow up. If you look at the unicorns that are being made on this planet right now, unicorns, billionaires, look at the billionaire companies. Look at the Facebook, okay? Mark Z, it's going to get $45 billion away. He just said this week, I'm going to give $45 billion worth of stock away before I die in my lifetime. He was not worried about the cost of pizza. Now, look, if you're starving, if you're starving, then you're worried about the price of a pizza. But you should be worried about what? The price of a pizza or your income? You should be focused on your income. The only reason somebody starts, the guy wrote me from India the other day, Mr. Grant, what do I do? I am over here, there's no money. I said, dude, move. If you got to move, you move, okay? If you got to leave, you leave. I live in America, okay? Conditions aren't great for people in America. The average America makes $52,000 a year. The average America makes $52,000 a year, where the average cost of living is higher than $52,000 a year. Do you understand that? 76% of all Americans live paycheck to paycheck in the wealthiest country in the world. It's no different in America than it is for a guy in India making $2 a day. No difference. Neither one of them, the guy in America or the guy in India, has enough income. There's not enough income. So all your attention is on, oh, what's the stool cost? I need to buy a stool. How much does it cost? This is not your problem. Your problem is, how do I get money so I can buy a bunch of stools and not worry about the cost of the stool? Do you understand? I was in a bookstore in Dallas, Texas. I was in the airport in Dallas, and I'm walking through, and my book, the 10X Rule, was in the bookstore. And a guy walks up to the book, and he looks at the 10X Rule, and he looks at the cover, and I can tell, dude, I got his attention already. The marketing and the branding worked. I got his attention. ## [03:01 - 04:45]

10X means explode 10 times, right? Then he turns it over. He turns it over. What's he looking at at the back of the book? How much is it? I walked up to him, I said, pal, that's not your problem. What's in that book is worth more money than that 29.95. You're not buying that book for 29.95. You're buying that book because you want to blow up. I wrote that book, and I guarantee you 10X is an underestimation of what it will do for you. It's going to explode your business. How many people do you have in your organization? 40. I said, good, let's get you 40 copies. Let's bring them to the counter. I'll pay the shipping, and I'll sign them all. He says, no, I'll tell you what I'll do. I'll take 10 books. And we sold him 10 books. So he went from being price-oriented, focused, obsessed, compulsive to, oh, no, I want to blow up. So you got to shift your attention, get it off of price. Every time, the next time, every time, every time you look at the price of the steak, the lobster, the drink, by the way, most people don't look at the price of their drink, they justify $14 for their martini, but then can't buy the dinner that they want. Okay, every time you complain about your phone bill, your gas bill, your oil bill, your heating bill, the blouse, that bag that your wife wanted to buy, every time you complain about it, understand this. You have your attention on the wrong thing. Price is not your problem. The cost of a $1,800 bag that makes your wife happy for months is not your problem. You know what your problem is? You don't make enough income. And any time you have your attention on a price tag, you have lost attention for what you really want. Looking at prices is an indication that you have contracted financially.
